task_description: Uninstall a software updater application from the system's installed apps settings.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows Start menu’s pinned apps area, with a semitransparent red X centered on the gray gear icon labeled “设置,” which is the Settings app. This is a UI control in the Start menu app grid. In the full-screen view, the Start menu is open in the center of the desktop, showing pinned applications and recommended items, while a Phone Link-style panel is open on the right with an iPhone device overview.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open Windows Settings from the Start menu by selecting the Settings app tile.
caption.action: Click the Settings app tile labeled “设置” with the gray gear icon.
caption.expectation: The Settings window should open immediately, replacing or appearing above the Start menu.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the left navigation sidebar of the Windows Settings app, with a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled “应用” (Apps). This is a sidebar navigation UI control, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen view, the Settings window is already open on the “应用 > 安装的应用” page, showing a list of installed programs on the right and the left sidebar with categories such as 系统, 蓝牙和其他设备, 网络和 Internet, 个性化, 应用, 账户, and others.
caption.think: The user is likely clicking the Apps category in the Settings sidebar to keep focus on or confirm the Apps section while viewing installed applications.
caption.action: Click the “应用” (Apps) item in the left Settings navigation sidebar.
caption.expectation: The Apps section should become selected or remain selected, and the main panel should stay on the Apps area, showing the installed apps list without opening a separate dialog.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a light gray blank strip between app-setting cards in the main content area; there is no button label, icon, text field, handle, or other interactive control directly under the X. This indicates a click on a blank region rather than a UI control or canvas object. In the full-screen view, the Windows Settings app is open to “应用” (Apps). The left sidebar has categories such as 主页, 系统, 蓝牙和其他设备, 网络和 Internet, 个性化, 应用, 账户, and others, while the main panel lists app-related options like “安装的应用,” “高级应用设置,” “默认应用,” and more. The X is in empty space in the main panel, not on any listed option.
caption.think: The user likely intends to click an empty area of the Apps settings page, possibly to remove focus from any item or simply continue without selecting a specific setting. Since the click is on blank space, it should not open a menu or navigate anywhere.
caption.action: Click the blank light gray area in the main Apps settings panel.
caption.expectation: Nothing major should change; any existing focus may clear, and the Apps page should remain visible without opening a new page, dialog, or menu.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the entire Windows Settings window without any semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click. The page title at the top of the main panel is “应用 > 启动,” and the visible list contains startup apps such as Intel Graphics Command Center Startup Task, Lenovo Hotkeys, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Edge, Microsoft OneDrive, Power Automate Desktop, Realtek HD Audio Universal Service, and Steam++ System Boot Run, each with status text and toggle switches on the right. In the full-screen view, the Settings app is open with the left sidebar on “应用,” and the right-side scrollbar is positioned partway down the startup apps list, showing that the main content panel is scrollable.
caption.think: The user likely wants to continue moving downward through the Startup apps list to see more entries below the currently visible ones in the main panel.
caption.action: Scroll down in the Startup apps list within the main Settings content area.
caption.expectation: The list should move upward, revealing additional startup app entries farther down the page while keeping the Settings window on “应用 > 启动.”

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the left navigation sidebar of the Windows Settings app with a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled “应用” (Apps). This is a sidebar navigation control, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, Settings is open to “应用 > 启动,” showing the Startup apps list in the main panel, and the “应用” section is already the active category in the left sidebar.
caption.think: The user is clicking the Apps category in the Settings sidebar, likely to keep focus on or reconfirm the Apps section while staying within the current Startup settings area.
caption.action: Click the “应用” (Apps) item in the left Settings navigation sidebar.
caption.expectation: The Apps category should remain selected, and the window should stay within the Apps section, likely continuing to show the current Startup page without opening a separate dialog.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the first card in the Apps settings list, specifically over the entry labeled “安装的应用” with the subtitle “卸载和管理电脑上的应用.” This is a clickable list item control, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the Windows Settings app is open to “应用” (Apps), with the left sidebar highlighting “应用,” and the main panel listing options such as “安装的应用,” “高级应用设置,” “默认应用,” and others.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the Installed apps page to view, manage, or uninstall applications on the computer.
caption.action: Click the “安装的应用” (Installed apps) list item in the main Apps settings panel.
caption.expectation: The Settings window should navigate to the Installed apps page, showing the list of apps installed on the PC.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the entire Windows Settings window with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action. The page header reads “应用 > 安装的应用,” and the main content area displays a vertically scrollable list of installed programs, including entries such as “开始体验”应用, 《使命召唤：现代战争® II 2022》, 360压缩, Anaconda3 2024.10-1 (Python 3.12.7 64-bit), Android Studio, Apple Mobile Device Support, Apple Software Update, Application Insights Tools for Visual Studio 2015, Bitz Net 1.5.4, BlueStacks, and Bonjour. Each row has an app icon, app name, publisher/date details, size text on some rows, and a three-dot more-options button on the right. In the full-screen view, the Settings app is open with the left sidebar on “应用,” and the installed apps list occupies the main panel, showing that this central list is the scrollable region.
caption.think: The user likely wants to continue browsing further down the Installed apps list to find additional applications not currently visible.
caption.action: Scroll down in the main “安装的应用” (Installed apps) list.
caption.expectation: The installed apps list should move upward, revealing more app entries farther down while staying on the same “应用 > 安装的应用” page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the three-dot ellipsis button at the far right of the row labeled “Apple Software Update,” whose size reads “4.05 MB.” This is a UI control within that app’s list row, specifically the more-options button. In the full-screen view, Windows Settings is open to “应用 > 安装的应用” (Apps > Installed apps), showing a scrollable list of installed programs such as Apple Mobile Device Support, Apple Software Update, Application Insights Tools for Visual Studio 2015, Bitz Net 1.5.4, BlueStacks, and Bonjour, each with a three-dot menu button on the right.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the context menu for the “Apple Software Update” entry so they can manage that app, such as uninstalling or viewing advanced options.
caption.action: Click the three-dot more-options button on the “Apple Software Update” row.
caption.expectation: A small context menu should open next to that button, showing available actions for the selected app entry.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a small context menu opened from the app row, with two vertically stacked options. The semitransparent red X is centered on the lower menu item labeled “卸载,” which is the Uninstall command. This is a UI control in a pop-up menu, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, Windows Settings is open to “应用 > 安装的应用” (Apps > Installed apps). The context menu is attached to the “Apple Software Update” entry, whose row shows size “4.05 MB,” and the menu currently lists “修改” above and “卸载” below.
caption.think: The user intends to remove Apple Software Update by choosing the Uninstall option from its three-dot menu.
caption.action: Click the “卸载” (Uninstall) menu item for “Apple Software Update.”
caption.expectation: The pop-up menu should close and Windows should open an uninstall confirmation prompt or begin the app’s uninstall flow for Apple Software Update.
